page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server configured with an AMD EPYC 9254 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. The system features 48 dedicated physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.9 GHz, with one thread per core. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, which provides 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a 960 GB local SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptography extensions such as AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates strong processing and memory capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and bzip3 decompression single-threaded performance both rank in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores place in the top 25%. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier PassMark memory scores, with uncached read bandwidth at 296.64 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 164.02 GB/sec. For database workloads, the server processes 72.25 million Redis SET operations per second and scores in the top 25% for PassMark database operations. Large language model inference using a small 135M model reaches top-tier speeds of 4386.48 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 390.91 tokens/sec for text generation. However, static web serving throughput is moderate, placing in the middle 50% at 42.35 GB/sec.

This bare metal configuration offers high resource density, combining dedicated physical cores with high-speed DDR5 memory. The absence of virtualization overhead and the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio make it a cost-efficient platform for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. It is suited for database hosting, memory-cached applications, data processing pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 9254 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, a 960 GB local SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and uncached read bandwidth reaching 296.64 GB/sec. The server handles 72.25 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. With a 1:8 core-to-RAM ratio and dedicated hardware, this server is optimized for memory-intensive databases, data processing, and CPU-based inference workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining forty-eight AMD EPYC cores with high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database and compute workloads.
